summary: Good soundboard recording captures an enthusiastic first‑show vibe, highlighted by a stellar opening of "Feel Like A Stranger" and strong jams through Terrapin Station, Space and "The Last Time > Days Between".
review: The show is remembered fondly as many fans’ first Dead concert, with the opener "Feel Like A Stranger" setting an upbeat tone and extended jams on Terrapin Station, Space and the seamless "The Last Time > Days Between" sequence earning high praise. Recording quality is described as good soundboard sound, though one reviewer disliked Hornsby’s accordion interludes. Overall the performance shines despite a single negative comment about keyboard instrumentation.

ai_rating.rationale: Average star rating ~4.2, strong positive comments on both show and recording; only one outlier low‑star review about Hornsby, so confidence is high.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers note good sound quality with clear mix; no major dropouts or balance issues mentioned.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans highlight a welcoming opening with "Feel Like A Stranger" followed by diverse, jam‑heavy selections; the space segment and unique song pairings (e.g., The Last Time > Days Between) are praised for flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers mention Jerry’s presence as a highlight, noting his on‑stage sit‑in and the excitement of first‑time listeners.
band_member_comments.Keys: Mixed: some praise Vince Welnick’s era, while one reviewer criticizes Hornsby’s accordion playing as detracting from songs.
